metric_definitions.mrr_at_8: Mean Reciprocal Rank over top 8 results. For each query, score = 1/rank if expected URI appears within first 8 results else 0; aggregate as arithmetic mean across queries.
metric_definitions.recall_at_8: Fraction of queries whose expected URI appears within the top 8 returned results.
metric_definitions.p95_ms: 95th percentile of per-query wall-clock latency in milliseconds (asyncio.perf_counter)
metric_definitions.avg_ms: Arithmetic mean of per-query latency in milliseconds

query_set.rationale: Small representative set spanning factual lookups, multi-token retrieval, and disambiguation. Each query has exactly one expected URI seeded in the test database. Designed to be deterministic and reproducible without external datasets.
query_set.notes: Queries reuse the same shape as backend/tests/fixtures/memory_gold_set.jsonl (id, query, expected_memory_uri).

semantic.note: Falls back to keyword path when embedding backend is disabled. Will report degraded=true and degrade_reasons in metadata.

regression_guidance.rrf_change: After enabling Reciprocal Rank Fusion, re-run search_quality_baseline.py. Compare new metrics against this baseline: mrr_at_8 and recall_at_8 must not decrease beyond noise; p95_ms must remain within 2x for the same query set and seed data.
regression_guidance.entity_rerank_change: After enabling entity-based reranking, expect mrr_at_8 to improve on multi-entity queries. Capture both new metrics and per-query deltas; do not accept overall regression on recall_at_8.
regression_guidance.tolerance: Treat differences below 0.02 (2 percentage points) in mrr_at_8 / recall_at_8 as noise unless deterministic seeding is verified.

notes: Baseline before RRF implementation. Null values to be filled by running search_quality_baseline.py against a seeded database. The script can be invoked via 'python -m pytest backend/tests/benchmark/search_quality_baseline.py'. The 'measure' test writes the live metrics back into this JSON file in-place under baseline_metrics.*.
